page: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with 16 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6448H processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 physical cores with single-thread execution per core and shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 180.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.25 GB per core. For acceleration, it includes a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture with 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1200 GB NVMe SSD.

Benchmark results highlight the server's capabilities, particularly in machine learning workloads. In LLM inference tests using a 7B model, the system achieved top-tier performance with prompt processing speeds of 6293.73 tokens per second and text generation speeds of 153.1 tokens per second. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also reached top-tier levels at 35.53 GB/sec and 15.59 GB/sec respectively. Standard CPU and memory benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU, scored in the average tier, reflecting its shared CPU allocation model.

This server offers a specialized balance of high-capacity GPU VRAM and dense system memory, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require substantial GPU acceleration without the overhead of dedicated multi-GPU setups. The combination of 48 GB VRAM and 180 GB system memory is highly suited for large language model inference, medium-scale AI training, data science pipelines, and memory-intensive graphics rendering.
description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server designed for accelerated workloads. It features 16 vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6448H processor, 180.0 GB of system memory, and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM on the Ada Lovelace architecture. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in LLM inference, reaching 6293.73 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model, alongside top-tier single-threaded compression speeds. While CPU compute benchmarks fall into the average tier due to shared allocation, the high VRAM and system memory density make this instance highly efficient for machine learning inference, data science, and graphics rendering workloads.
og_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, a 1200 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for accelerated AI and inference workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-l40s-16c-180g-48vram is a Cloud GPU server with 16 vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for AI inference.
tagline: An accelerated cloud instance combining a 48 GB L40S GPU, dense system memory, and NVMe storage for demanding AI inference workloads.

16 Intel Xeon 6448H vCPUs with 180 GB of system memory
